hook.scenario: Imagine two developers: Dev A can finish an API in 10 days, Dev B in 15 days. If they pair program, how fast does it ship? This is the exact math tech companies test you on.

mental_model.intuition: Work = Time * Rate. Instead of adding days, add their per-day speed (rates).
mental_model.description: Never add time directly. If A takes 10 days and B takes 15 days, they don't take 25 days together! Convert everything to 'work done per day' (efficiency) and add those.

pattern_discovery.rule: To find combined time, add their individual rates (reciprocals of time), then flip it back.

formula.statement: If A can do work in 'x' days and B in 'y' days, together they take (x*y)/(x+y) days.
formula.explain: This is just the algebraic simplification of 1/(1/x + 1/y). For 3 people, stick to the LCM method instead of complex formulas.

shortcuts.title: The LCM (Chocolate) Method
shortcuts.trick: Assume total work = LCM of given days. Calculate chocolates eaten per day.
shortcuts.example: A=10 days, B=15 days. LCM=30 chocolates. A eats 3/day, B eats 2/day. Together they eat 5/day. 30/5 = 6 days.

common_mistakes.title: Adding Days Directly
common_mistakes.explanation: Assuming A(10) + B(15) = 25 days. More people means LESS time, not more.

recall_questions.q: If P takes 20 days and Q takes 30 days, what is their combined time using the formula (x*y)/(x+y)?
What is the assumed total work (LCM) for 12 days, 15 days, and 20 days?
If A completes 1/3 of the work in 5 days, how many days for the full work?

oa_questions.question: A can do a piece of work in 8 days. B is 3 times as efficient as A. In how many days can they complete it together?
oa_questions.approach: A's rate = 1 unit/day (takes 8 days, total work = 8). B's rate = 3 units/day. Together = 4 units/day. Time = 8 / 4 = 2 days.

oa_questions.question: A takes 15 days and B takes 20 days. They work together for 4 days, then A leaves. How many days will B take to finish the remaining work?
oa_questions.approach: Total work = LCM(15,20) = 60 units. A=4/day, B=3/day. In 4 days, they do (4+3)*4 = 28 units. Remaining = 32 units. B takes 32/3 = 10.67 days.
